name: Gir National Park
description: Last wild Asiatic lions on Earth (674 lions as of 2020 census, only in Gir). Sasan Gir village is the base. Jeep safari (Gir main zone, permit from Junagadh wildlife office, ₹3000-5000). Lion show (near entrance, guaranteed lion sighting on raised platform, controversial). 1,412 sq km. Also has leopards, spotted deer, sambar. Diu (90km beach resort) nearby. Airport: Rajkot (RAJ) 160km, Diu (DIU) 90km. This location offers a unique blend of experiences that captivate travelers from around the world. Whether you are looking for relaxation, adventure, or cultural immersion, this destination has something special to offer. The local hospitality is warm and welcoming, ensuring a memorable stay. We recommend planning your trip well in advance to make the most of the diverse attractions. The scenic beauty, historical significance, and vibrant local life make it a must-visit spot on any itinerary. Don't forget to explore the hidden gems and interact with the locals to truly understand the essence of this wonderful place.
history: The history of Gir National Park is deeply rooted in ancient traditions and significant historical events that shaped the region. Over the centuries, it has witnessed the rise and fall of various empires, each leaving its indelible mark on the local culture and architecture.
nearestBusStand: Gir National Park Central Bus Stand

culture: The culture of Gir National Park is a vibrant tapestry of indigenous traditions, colonial influences, and modern adaptations, reflecting a diverse community.
travelTips: Stay hydrated and carry a water bottle.
Use local transport for an authentic experience.
weather: Pleasant weather throughout most of the year, with heavy rainfall during monsoons.
